Serious Concern Regarding Incorrect Auto-Linked Bank Account I recently noticed that my INDmoney account is showing a bank account number and IFSC code that are completely unknown to me. I have never added, authorized, or linked this account at any point. This appears to have been auto-linked by the system, which raises serious concerns about account security, data accuracy, and fund safety. I hope the support team resolves this matter urgently and transparently.

great App's excellent work

Everything in the app is pretty good and smooth in terms of performance, but the recent UI update is not very appealing. The previous UI felt better and more intuitive. Earlier, it was easier to view the portfolio in formats like K, L, and Cr, which made it more user-friendly. Also, the goal-based investment view was clearer and more accessible before.

Dear INDmoney, you’ve made instant withdrawal feel like magic! 💸 No waiting, no tension—just one tap and money lands in my bank. Your smooth trading experience + superfast Insta-Withdrawal = pure love. Best decision ever to trade with you. Thank you for treating my money with such speed and care. You’ve earned a lifelong fan!

I really enjoy using the app, but the UPI payment experience needs refinement. Currently, it feels like an afterthought rather than a core feature. To improve this, I suggest integrating a more seamless interface and adding fingerprint authentication for faster, more secure UPI transactions.

This was my goto app for investing, but it seems like I'm the only one experiencing a glitch with a simple toggle button. Have been writing so many emails, but I'm constantly met with "we are checking with our concerned team". What really ticked me was they lied to me about the toggle button and mentioned it was removed during a recent update. Checked with my friends, and they all continue to have the option. Toggle button missing may seem trivial, but Ind's UI is what attracted me.

INDmoney offers useful features, but constant UI/UX changes make it frustrating to use. Frequent updates shift menus and navigation, forcing users to relearn the app again and again. Instead of improving usability, many changes create confusion and slow down simple tasks. A finance app should feel stable and predictable. Please focus on consistency and meaningful improvements rather than frequent redesigns.

Nice

The USD conversion via ICICI Bank is misleading. The exchange rate shown in the INDMoney app is not the rate actually applied—there’s a difference of about ₹0.30 per dollar, with the bank charging a higher rate. When I contacted ICICI Bank, they stated that since this is a third-party transaction, they cannot address the discrepancy. On the other hand, INDMoney’s support team was unable to provide a clear explanation for the difference in exchange rates.

Excellent functionality and integration. However, the following key functionality is missing in the portfolio section: It does not show YTD gain and CAGR of stocks purchased from other brokers. INDMoney has information about amount and current price from the depository so letting users manually add purchase date and buy price would enable this feature.

Unreasonably high MTF charges compared to competitors. INDmoney charges 0.1% uncapped brokerage on MTF trades. On a ₹7.7L trade, I was charged around ₹775 for a single order. plus 1k of additional charges (approx)Meanwhile, platforms like Zerodha cap brokerage at around ₹20 per order while offering similar or better margin benefits. If any one are planning to use MTF, think twice. This pricing model can quietly eat into your capital. these guys are looting like anything
